title: Breaking cut: skills binary rename, install.sh, shell tests, docs
description:
Type: HITL (one manual TUI launch in a real terminal; everything else automated). Plan slice 9 — the single commit that closes the breaking-cut PR; all shell suites must return green here. Shell tests first (red): test-install.sh → install --all / install --all --force / uninstall --all with explicit exit-status capture under set -euo pipefail (blocked case now exits 1), fake-go build command 'build -o bin/skills .', bin/skills assertion, go.sum-staleness rebuild case, list+doctor smoke checks; test-hooks-install.sh (4 invocations) and test-forked-skills-install.sh (--all + --none) migrated the same way. Then green: install.sh builds bin/skills, staleness find includes go.sum, header comment updated. Docs: AGENTS.md Installation+Verification; README.md flag docs (:89, :106, :109-111 on origin/main@391bdc6 — re-verify) → subcommand equivalents; CLAUDE.md is a symlink, edit AGENTS.md only. Backstop grep sweep: rg -n -- 'skills-tui|--all|--none|--force' README.md AGENTS.md install.sh scripts/ and disposition every hit.

User stories: 6.
acceptance_criteria: All five verification scripts exit 0 (test-skills-tui-go.sh, test-install.sh, test-forked-skills-install.sh, test-hooks-install.sh, test-forked-skills-layout.sh); grep sweep dispositioned; manual ./install.sh TUI launch verified in a real terminal

title: skills doctor command (text + --json)
description:
Type: AFK. Plan slice 8. Part of the single breaking-cut PR. doctorCmd = Discover + Doctor + renderers (reuse the table writer extracted by the list issue). Clean → 'no problems found', exit 0; findings → one line per finding grouped by check, exit 1; --json emits the documented Finding schema (skill populated for any finding attributable to a discovered skill).

User stories: 4, 5.
acceptance_criteria: Clean fixture exits 0; planted dangling link + stale staged copy + hook drift fixture exits 1 with grouped output; --json round-trips schema; go suite green

title: skills install / skills uninstall commands
description:
Type: AFK. Plan slice 7. Part of the single breaking-cut PR. Shared helper: resolve names (or full list for --all), snapshot states, build []ApplyPlan, ApplyAllResults(plans, force, w); exit 1 on any Blocked/Partial outcome, Err != nil, or resolve error. Validate(): bare command or names+--all → usage error exit 2, nothing touched. install --force uses the destroy-plan path (no bypass loop; already-installed = no-op even under --force). uninstall keeps ownership rules (foreign symlinks survive). applyNoninteractive is deleted; port its old main_test.go assertions. Targets honored; hooks not target-gated; skipped-team sub-case needs SKILL_INSTALL_TARGETS=agents with a Claude-only team (under targets=claude nothing is skipped).

User stories: 2, 3, 5.
acceptance_criteria: Full slice-7 test table passes (per-name install, blocked exit 1, force overwrite, force-failure exit 1, unknown/ambiguous names, foreign-symlink survival, failing-uninstall exit 1, targets gating, skipped teams); go suite green

title: skills list command (text + --json)
description:
Type: AFK. Plan slice 6. Part of the single breaking-cut PR. listCmd = Discover + Report per skill; text: one aligned line per skill '<name> <kind> <state>' in Discover order; --json emits the documented schema (incl. orphan target rows and skipped states). Runtime error (unreadable repo) → exit 1. Extract the text table writer for reuse by the doctor command.

User stories: 1, 5.
acceptance_criteria: list text and --json tests pass against temp repo+HOME fixture; --json round-trips the documented schema; exit codes 0/1 verified; go suite green

title: Doctor engine: hook settings drift + repo-side validation (4c/4d)
description:
Type: AFK. Plan slice 4 sub-slices 4c+4d. 4c: settings entry missing / similar-but-different command (show both strings) / hook script symlink dangling or pointing outside staged copy — reuses hookScriptTargetState + hookSettingsEntryPresent, hooks never target-gated. 4d: duplicate names across kinds (one finding, empty skill field, detail names every kind/name); hooks/<dir> missing install.sh or hook.json (rescan hooks/ directly); first-party skill missing shared/ or runtimes/{claude,codex}/SKILL.md — cursor overlays are OPTIONAL (isForkedSkill requires claude+codex only), absence never a finding, a present cursor overlay missing SKILL.md is. Acceptance test: Doctor against the real repo checkout returns zero repo findings.

User stories: 4.
acceptance_criteria: 4c fixtures pass; 4d fixtures pass incl. cursor-optional rule; real-repo acceptance test returns zero repo findings; go suite green

title: Doctor engine: scaffold + target-link health + staged-copy drift (4a/4b)
description:
Type: AFK. Plan slice 4 sub-slices 4a+4b. Add internal/skills/doctor.go: Finding{Check,Skill,Path,Detail}, Config.Doctor(list) []Finding, stable order. 4a target-link health over SkillLinks (target-gated) distinguishing dangling / foreign-live / owned-legacy ('legacy link; reinstall migrates it', ownedSources) / forked-orphan (NOT target-gated, links.go orphan rule); TargetCopy and healthy states are non-findings. Dedup rule: staged-drift suppresses per-target stale findings for the same staged copy (fixture: third-party skill staled across three roots → exactly one finding). 4b staged-copy drift + orphans across ALL stage roots incl. runtimes/<rt>/agent-teams/; backups/ ignored; legacy carve-outs: skills/<name> (forked portable) AND flat agent-teams/<teamDir> (forked teams, legacyTeamOwnedSources) report staged-drift, never orphan; runtime-leftover staged copies for dropped overlays are orphans attributed to the skill. Gating fixture per plan (SKILL_INSTALL_TARGETS=claude, cursor-overlay vs cursor-less skill).

User stories: 4.
acceptance_criteria: doctor_test.go fixtures for all 4a classes + dedup + gating pass; 4b fixtures incl. forked-team drift and both legacy carve-outs pass; go suite green

title: Library: ApplyAllResults with error capture and destroy passthrough
description:
Type: AFK. Plan slice 3. Extend internal/skills/plan.go: ApplyAllResults(plans, destroy, w) []ApplyResult — identical printed output to ApplyAll today; entries only for executed actions (ActionNone omitted); destroy=true overwrites stale real dirs via the upgrade action (replaces the legacy --force bypass loop's behavior). ApplyResult gains Err error (failing uninstall still prints '- removed' parity line but carries Err; same for unexpected install errors). ApplyAll becomes a wrapper deriving its changed bool from the results (TestApplyAllNothingToDo must stay green).

User stories: 2, 3, 5.
acceptance_criteria: Output-equality test vs ApplyAll passes; destroy=true overwrite test passes; failing-uninstall fixture yields Err != nil with parity output; TUI call sites untouched

title: Kong CLI skeleton: parser, TUI default command, exit-code contract
description:
Type: AFK. Plan slice 5. Part of the single breaking-cut PR (with list/install/uninstall/doctor commands and the final migration): shell suites driving ./install.sh are expected-red from here until the migration issue lands. Add github.com/alecthomas/kong (pinned tag; first real third-party dep — govulncheck now scans it). CLI struct with Repo + hidden default Tui command only; subcommands are added incrementally by their own issues (no stubs). Panic-sentinel kong.Exit + kong.Writers so run() stays in-process testable; parse errors → exit 2; repo-resolution failure and missing HOME → exit 2; keep exit 130 TUI SIGINT carve-out and TestCLIInterruptedTUIExitCode. Delete parseFlags/usageText; new non-TTY message references subcommands.

User stories: 5, 6.
acceptance_criteria: --help exits 0 without invoking runTUI (pins sentinel design); unknown command exit 2; TTY stub launches TUI via default command; exit-130 test green; go suite green

title: Library: structured SkillReport + SkillState aggregation unification
description:
Type: AFK. Plan slice 2. Add internal/skills/report.go: Config.Report(skill) returning SkillReport{Name,Kind,State,Targets[{Path,Status}]} with JSON tags per the epic's list --json schema — including hook rows (script target mapped fresh→linked/differ→stale/missing + settings:<file> present|absent), skipped teams, and forked-orphan links as status=orphan (state.go forkedOrphanTargets; NOT target-gated). Invariant test: Report(s).State == SkillState(s) for every fixture including orphan + skipped. Refactor SkillState to consume the same collected statuses (single aggregation implementation); TUI behavior unchanged (state_test.go is the regression net).

User stories: 1, 5.
acceptance_criteria: report_test.go passes incl. orphan fixture keeping StateUpgrade alive; JSON golden matches documented schema; all existing state_test.go tests untouched and green

title: Library: name resolution (Resolve) with kind/name disambiguation
description:
Type: AFK. Plan slice 1 (see epic as-zvu design field). Add internal/skills/resolve.go: Resolve(list []Skill, names []string) ([]Skill, []errs) supporting bare names and kind/name prefixes (first|third|team|hook; team covers KindTeam and KindTeamHybrid, names are trimmed team names like go-review). Typed UnknownNameError / AmbiguousNameError — all resolve errors are exit-1 class for the CLI. TDD: resolve_test.go table test first (unique, kind-qualified, ambiguous cross-kind duplicate, unknown, malformed kind, multi-arg order + dedupe).

User stories: 2, 3.
acceptance_criteria: resolve_test.go covers all table cases and passes; scripts/test-skills-tui-go.sh green; no existing test touched

title: Skills CLI + shared installer library
description:
One binary (renamed 'skills') serving the existing TUI plus new CLI subcommands (list, install <name...>, uninstall <name...>, doctor) over a shared engine: the internal/skills package extended in place. Kong parsing with the TUI as default command; text + --json output on list/doctor; strict exit codes (0 success / 1 findings-failures / 2 usage, 130 TUI SIGINT carve-out); legacy --all/--none/--force flags removed with install.sh, all shell suites, README, and AGENTS.md migrated in the same change.

Full reviewed implementation plan (5 review loops, 9/10 gate) is in the design field. Plan slices 1-4 (library) are independently mergeable; plan slices 5-9 (CLI + breaking cut) must ship as ONE PR because the install.sh-driving shell suites are red in between.

User stories:
1. See the install state of every skill from the command line (list)
2. Install/upgrade specific skills by name (install, --force)
3. Uninstall specific skills by name, non-destructively (uninstall)
4. Diagnose install health without changing anything (doctor)
5. Script/agent consumers get --json and strict exit codes
6. ./install.sh and its test suite keep working after the flag migration
design:
# Plan: Shared installer library + `skills` CLI alongside the TUI

## Goal

Extend `tools/skills-tui` so one binary (renamed `skills`) serves two frontends over one
shared engine:

- **TUI** (unchanged behavior): `skills` with no subcommand launches the existing
  interactive installer.
- **CLI** (new): `skills list`, `skills install <name>...|--all [--force]`,
  `skills uninstall <name>...|--all`, `skills doctor`, with `--json` on `list` and
  `doctor`, strict exit codes, and Kong-based parsing.

The shared library is the existing `internal/skills` package, extended in place
(name resolution, structured reports, apply-result collection, doctor checks). The
legacy top-level flags (`--all`, `--none`, `--force`) are removed; `install.sh`, the
shell test suite, README.md, and AGENTS.md migrate in the same change.

## Non-goals

- No exported/public Go API; `internal/skills` stays internal to the module.
- No new TUI features; the TUI is only touched where the entry point changes.
- No shell completion, no man pages, no config file.
- No interactive confirmation prompts; `--force` remains the only destructive gate.
- Doctor does not *fix* anything (read-only), and does not replace
  `scripts/test-forked-skills-layout.sh` (CI's repo lint remains authoritative).
- No CI workflow expansion (hygiene.yml only runs the layout lint today; adding the Go
  suite to CI is a separate task).
- No bash-output parity requirement for the new subcommands: `install --all --force`
  may print plan-based status lines (`^ upgraded ...`) instead of the legacy bare
  `+ <name>` lines, because every consumer of the old output is migrated in slice 9.
  TUI output parity IS preserved (its code paths don't change).

## Interview decisions (fixed constraints)

| Decision | Choice |
|---|---|
| v1 commands | `list`, `install <name...>`, `uninstall <name...>`, `doctor` |
| Packaging | One binary, subcommands; no args → TUI |
| Binary name | `skills` (built at `tools/skills-tui/bin/skills`); dir/module unchanged |
| Legacy flags | Removed entirely; consumers migrated in the same change |
| Output | Text default; `--json` on `list` and `doctor` |
| Exit codes | 0 success/clean · 1 findings/failed skills · 2 usage errors |
| Library | Extend `internal/skills`; no facade package |
| Parsing | Kong (`github.com/alecthomas/kong`); TUI as default command |
| Bare `install`/`uninstall` | Usage error (exit 2); names or `--all` required |
| Doctor checks | Target-link health, staged-copy drift, hook settings drift, repo validation |
| Hooks | Addressable by name like any skill; not gated on `SKILL_INSTALL_TARGETS` |

## Current system observations

**Observation base: `origin/main` @ 391bdc6** (the plan was initially drafted against
a stale `wire-hooks-into-tui` checkout — since squash-merged as a98b910/#71 — and
re-verified against origin/main; notable drift absorbed below: `feature-review-team`
and `go-review-team` are now runtime-forked with staged copies under
`runtimes/<rt>/agent-teams/` (6d13134/#74), `.beads/` is tracked content (#80), and
README's flag docs moved). Line refs are anchors, not gospel: the executor should
re-verify any ref that doesn't match after branching.

- `tools/skills-tui/main.go:66` — hand-rolled `parseFlags` for `--all/--none/--force/--repo`;
  `run()` at `main.go:99` assembles `skills.Config` and dispatches to
  `applyNoninteractive` or `tui.Run`. `main_test.go` tests this seam with injected
  stdout/stderr/getenv/isTTY — the same seam the CLI tests will use.
- `internal/skills` is already UI-free: `Discover` (`discover.go:17`) returns
  `[]Skill{Kind, Name, Source, Forked, Hook}`; team names are the directory basename
  with `-team` trimmed (`discover.go:60`) — e.g. `agent-teams/go-review-team` is skill
  `go-review`; `Config.SkillState` (`state.go:72`) aggregates per-target `targetState`
  (`state.go:28`, unexported) into installed/upgrade/partial/not-installed;
  `ApplySkill`/`ApplyAll` (`plan.go:92`, `plan.go:163`) execute and print bash-parity
  status lines; `hookState` (`hooks.go:142`) classifies hook script symlink + settings
  entry (its own `hookScriptState` enum: missing/fresh/differ); `parseHookManifest`
  (`hooks.go:232`) validates hook.json.
- `targetState` collapses dangling symlinks into `TargetForeign` (`state.go:16`) — doctor
  needs finer detail (dangling vs foreign-live vs owned-legacy) and must derive it
  itself via `Readlink` + `Stat`. Two link classes live OUTSIDE the simple
  linked/foreign split and must not be mislabeled: (i) **owned-legacy links** —
  repo-pointing or legacy-staged-pointing symlinks the installer treats as its own
  and migrates without force (`ownedSources`, `links.go:389`; AGENTS.md: "Existing
  repo-pointing symlinks are treated as upgradeable"); (ii) **forked-orphan links** —
  links at targets `SkillLinks` no longer yields (e.g. a leftover `~/.cursor` link
  for a now cursor-less skill) that `SkillState` counts via `forkedOrphanTargets`
  and that force `StateUpgrade` even on otherwise-skipped skills. Note
  `links.go:403`: "Orphans are reported even when the runtime root is not in
  SKILL_INSTALL_TARGETS" — orphan detection is deliberately NOT target-gated.
- `ApplyAll` (`plan.go:163`) prints but does not return per-skill results, hardcodes
  `destroy=false`, and `ApplySkill`'s remove branch always reports `OutcomeRemoved`
  (real uninstall errors go only to `WarnW`) — the CLI needs results **including
  errors** and a destroy passthrough to honor the exit-code contract (slices 3, 7).
- Cursor overlays are optional by design: `isForkedSkill` (`discover.go:106`)
  requires claude+codex only, and the layout lint
  (`scripts/test-forked-skills-layout.sh:71`) enforces cursor-less *teams*;
  `product-manager` remains cursor-less in fact. Doctor's repo validation must
  mirror the claude+codex-only rule.
- Teams are runtime-forked too (6d13134): their staged copies live under
  `runtimes/<rt>/agent-teams/<teamDir>` via `RuntimeTeamStagedSource`
  (`stage.go:48`) — a staging root any stage-dir scan must include alongside
  `skills/`, `runtimes/<rt>/skills/`, `agent-teams/`, and `hooks/`.
- Module deps: only `golang.org/x/term` (+ indirect `x/sys`). Kong is the first real
  third-party dependency. `scripts/test-skills-tui-go.sh` runs gofmt/vet/build/test and
  best-effort `govulncheck`, which will now scan Kong too.
- `install.sh` builds `bin/skills-tui` and execs it with `--repo "$REPO_DIR" "$@"`.
  The staleness check (`find ... -newer "$BIN"`) covers `*.go` and `go.mod` — **not**
  `go.sum`; adding Kong makes go.sum load-bearing, so the find pattern must include it.
- Legacy-flag consumers (verified on origin/main): `scripts/test-install.sh`
  (`--all` :47, `--force` :71, `--none` :128, and a fake `go` asserting the literal
  build command `build -o bin/skills-tui .` :96),
  `scripts/test-forked-skills-install.sh` (`--all` :32 AND `--none` :146 with
  assertion text :147-149), `scripts/test-hooks-install.sh` (`--all` :46/:61/:71,
  `--none` :75), and **README.md** (`--force` in the TUI-apply description :89;
  non-interactive flag docs :109-111; `--all` example :106). CI (`hygiene.yml`) only
  runs `test-forked-skills-layout.sh` — no CI edits needed. The slice-9 grep sweep
  is the completeness backstop for this inventory.
- Git state: `wire-hooks-into-tui` merged as a98b910 (#71); **this work starts from
  latest `origin/main` on a new branch.**
- Skill names are directory basenames across `skills/`, `third-party/`, `hooks/`, and
  trimmed team names from `agent-teams/`; uniqueness is not enforced anywhere.

## CLI contract (the spec the tests encode)

```
skills                        → TUI (requires TTY on stdin+stdout, as today)
skills list [--json]
skills install <name>... | --all   [--force]
skills uninstall <name>... | --all
skills doctor [--json]
```

- **Global**: `--repo <dir>` on every command (default: walk up from cwd for
  `skills/` + `AGENTS.md`, as today); env `SKILL_INSTALL_TARGETS`,
  `SKILL_SYMLINKS_DIR`, `HOME` guard — all identical to current behavior. The
  non-TTY message for the bare command becomes
  "Not a terminal. Use a subcommand (list, install, uninstall, doctor) for
  non-interactive use." (the old text references removed flags).
- **Name resolution**: bare names match `Skill.Name`. If a bare name matches skills of
  more than one kind, the command errors listing the matches and accepts
  `<kind>/<name>`, where kind ∈ `first|third|team|hook` and `team` covers both
  `KindTeam` and `KindTeamHybrid`. Unknown names and malformed kind prefixes are
  reported individually to stderr; remaining valid names are still processed; final
  exit code 1 (these are failed requests, not usage errors).
- **install**: plans per skill from a pre-apply state snapshot (same `ApplyPlan`
  gating as today). `--force` sets the destroy flag on the SAME plan-based path
  (`ApplySkill(s, desired, destroy=true)`), so blocked/stale real directories are
  overwritten via the upgrade action; there is no separate bypass-planning loop (the
  legacy `applyNoninteractive` force loop is deleted, its overwrite behavior covered
  by slice-7 tests and the migrated shell tests). Already-installed skills are a
  planned no-op even under `--force`. Skills whose runtime roots are all untargeted
  (`StateSkipped`) are reported as skipped and don't affect the exit code.
- **uninstall**: keeps the non-destructive ownership rules (only staged-pointing
  symlinks and our settings entries are removed).
- **Exit codes**:
  - `0` — everything requested succeeded / no findings.
  - `1` — any requested skill ended blocked or partial, any resolve error (unknown or
    ambiguous name), any apply/uninstall error recorded in `ApplyResult.Err`, any
    doctor finding, or any runtime failure (unreadable repo, etc.).
  - `2` — usage errors: unknown command/flag (Kong parse error), bare
    `install`/`uninstall` with neither names nor `--all` (enforced by the command's
    `Validate()`, since Kong cannot express "args or flag" natively), names combined
    with `--all`, repo resolution failure (either `--repo` pointing at a non-repo or
    the no-flag walk-up finding nothing — both usage-class), missing `HOME`.
  - `130` — TUI interrupted via Ctrl-C only (`exitInterrupted`, `main.go:20`); this
    existing carve-out is preserved verbatim, along with its test
    (`main_test.go` `TestCLIInterruptedTUIExitCode`). Subcommands have no SIGINT
    special-casing in v1.
- **`--json` schemas** (stable, documented in `--help`):
  - `list --json`: `[{"name","kind","state","targets":[{"path","status"}]}]`.
    `state` ∈ installed|not-installed|upgrade|partial|skipped. `status` for
    portable/team targets ∈ linked|missing|foreign|copy|stale|orphan — `orphan` rows
    are the `forkedOrphanTargets` links (`state.go:104`): targets outside the
    skill's current `SkillLinks` that still hold an installer-relevant symlink; they
    are how a skipped-or-installed skill with a leftover link reports `upgrade`, so
    skipped skills have empty `targets` ONLY when no orphan links exist. Hook rows
    have exactly two targets: the script path with status ∈ linked|missing|stale
    (mapped from `hookScriptState` fresh/missing/differ) and pseudo-path
    `settings:<file>` with status ∈ present|absent.
  - `doctor --json`: `[{"check","skill","path","detail"}]`
    (check ∈ target-link|staged-drift|hook-settings|repo; `skill` is populated
    whenever the finding is attributable to a discovered skill — including
    orphan-link and runtime-leftover-staged findings — and empty only for
    repo-level findings and stage-dir orphans matching no discovered skill).
- **Kong wiring** (explicit, testable design — no hand-waving):
  - Parser built per `run()` call with `kong.Writers(stdout, stderr)` and
    `kong.Exit(func(code int) { panic(kongExit{code}) })`; `run()` recovers
    `kongExit` and returns its code. This is required because Kong's `--help` path
    prints help then calls Exit(0) — with a no-op Exit func, control would fall
    through and dispatch the default (TUI) command. The panic/recover sentinel makes
    `--help` a clean exit 0 and is pinned by a test asserting `--help` exits 0 AND
    launches nothing (runTUI stub not called).
  - Parse errors (unknown command/flag) are returned as errors from `p.Parse`; `run()`
    prints them to stderr and returns 2.
  - The TUI is `Tui struct{} \`cmd:"" default:"1" hidden:""\``, so `skills` with no
    args selects it; `skills --repo X` (flag only) also selects it — matching today's
    behavior.

## Verification commands (discovered from the repo)

```bash
scripts/test-skills-tui-go.sh          # gofmt, go vet, go build, go test, govulncheck
scripts/test-install.sh                # ./install.sh end-to-end against temp HOME
scripts/test-forked-skills-install.sh  # runtime staging via ./install.sh
scripts/test-hooks-install.sh          # hook round-trip via ./install.sh (needs jq)
scripts/test-forked-skills-layout.sh   # repo lint (CI parity)
```

Evidence of success: all five exit 0; `go test ./...` green including the new
`resolve_test.go`, `report_test.go`, `doctor_test.go`, and rewritten `main_test.go`.

**Suite status between slices (explicit):** the Go suite
(`scripts/test-skills-tui-go.sh`) must be green at the end of every slice. The three
shell suites that drive `./install.sh` go RED at slice 5 (legacy flags deleted) and
return green at slice 9 — this is expected and acknowledged; slices 5–9 ship as one
PR and nothing lands on main in the interim. If an incremental-merge strategy is
wanted instead, slices 1–4 can merge alone (pure library additions, all suites green).

## Implementation plan — TDD tracer-bullet slices

Each slice: write the failing test, confirm the exact red state, implement to green,
refactor, run `scripts/test-skills-tui-go.sh` before moving on. Library slices (1–4)
land before any CLI slice; the consumer-visible breaking cut is confined to slices
5–9 within a single PR.

### Slice 0 — Branch setup (no test)

`git fetch origin && git switch -c skills-cli origin/main`. First action on the new
branch: spot-check the plan's cited line refs (they were verified at origin/main
@ 391bdc6); if main has moved again, re-verify the consumer inventory with the
slice-9 grep sweep before relying on it.

### Slice 1 — Name resolution (`internal/skills/resolve.go`)

- **Failing test** (`resolve_test.go`): table test over a fixture `[]Skill` containing
  a first-party, a third-party, a hook, a team (name `go-review`, exercising the
  trimmed-name form), and a deliberate cross-kind duplicate (`{first,"dup"}`,
  `{hook,"dup"}`). Cases: bare unique name → match; `first/dup` and `hook/dup` →
  match; `team/go-review` matches a `KindTeamHybrid` skill too; bare `dup` →
  `AmbiguousNameError` naming both kinds; unknown name → `UnknownNameError`;
  `bogus/tdd` (malformed kind) → error; multiple args resolve independently with
  order preserved and duplicate requests deduped.
- **Red**: package fails to compile (`Resolve` undefined).
- **Green**: `func Resolve(list []Skill, names []string) ([]Skill, []error)` with
  typed errors (`UnknownNameError`, `AmbiguousNameError`); all resolve errors are
  exit-1 class — only Kong parse errors and `Validate()` failures are exit 2.
- **Refactor**: kind-string mapping shared with the `Kind` constants; `team` maps to
  both team kinds.

### Slice 2 — Structured per-skill report (`internal/skills/report.go`)

- **Failing test** (`report_test.go`): using the package's existing temp-HOME fixture
  helpers (the `makeRepo`/`stageConfig`/`writeFile`-style helpers already used across
  the package tests — lift them into a shared `testsupport_test.go` if needed rather
  than duplicating), assert `Config.Report(skill)` returns
  `SkillReport{Name, Kind, State, Targets}` where `Targets` carries each link's path
  and status string; a hook skill yields exactly the script-target row (status mapped
  fresh→linked, differ→stale, missing→missing) plus the `settings:<file>` row
  (present|absent); a fully-untargeted team yields `State=skipped`, empty `Targets`;
  a forked-orphan fixture (cursor-less skill with a leftover owned `~/.cursor` link)
  yields an `orphan` target row AND `State=upgrade` — this is the fixture that keeps
  the `forkedOrphanTargets` differ++ behavior (`state.go:104`) alive through the
  refactor; JSON marshals with the documented field names (golden string).
- **Red**: `Report` undefined.
- **Green**: build on `SkillLinks` + `targetState` + `forkedOrphanTargets` (all stay
  unexported; `Report` lives in the same package). Invariant test: for EVERY fixture
  in this file — including the orphan and skipped ones —
  `Report(s).State == SkillState(s)`.
- **Refactor**: rewire `SkillState`'s aggregation loop (including its orphan
  injection) to consume the same collected per-target statuses `Report` gathers, so
  there is exactly one aggregation implementation (TUI behavior unchanged — existing
  `state_test.go` is the regression net).

### Slice 3 — Apply results with errors and destroy (`internal/skills/plan.go`)

- **Failing tests** (`plan_test.go` additions):
  - `ApplyAllResults(plans, destroy, w)` returns `[]ApplyResult` in order while
    writing the identical status block `ApplyAll` writes today (assert output
    equality against `ApplyAll` on a copied fixture, destroy=false). Membership
    rule: only executed actions produce entries — `ActionNone` plans are omitted
    (mirrors the printing loop; a no-op is a success and never affects exit codes).
  - With `destroy=true`, a stale real directory at a target is overwritten (upgrade
    action) — the behavior the legacy top-level `--force` provided via its bypass
    loop, now expressed through planning.
  - `ApplyResult` gains an `Err error` field: a failing uninstall (fixture: make the
    link's parent directory read-only) still prints `- removed <name>` (bash parity,
    warning to WarnW — both unchanged) but carries `Err != nil` so callers can
    exit 1. Same for unexpected (non-refusal) install errors.
- **Red**: `ApplyAllResults` undefined; `ApplyResult.Err` undefined.
- **Green**: extract `ApplyAll`'s loop body; `ApplyAll` becomes a wrapper over
  `ApplyAllResults(plans, false, w)` that derives its existing `changed` bool from
  the returned results (the bool is asserted by `TestApplyAllNothingToDo`,
  `review_fixes_test.go:319`). `ApplySkill` records the
  already-captured errors into `Err` instead of only logging them. TUI call sites and
  printed output untouched (existing tests prove it).
- **Refactor**: none expected.

### Slice 4 — Doctor engine (`internal/skills/doctor.go`), four sub-slices

Shared shape, tested first: `type Finding struct{ Check, Skill, Path, Detail string }`,
`func (c Config) Doctor(list []Skill) []Finding` running all checks in a stable order.
Each sub-slice adds one check with its own temp-HOME fixture test in `doctor_test.go`.

**Targets gating (explicit rule):** link-level checks over `SkillLinks` (4a i–v) and
hook checks (4c) inspect exactly what the installer manages: `SkillLinks` is already
gated on `SKILL_INSTALL_TARGETS`, so those problems in untargeted roots are out of
scope by design; hooks are checked unconditionally (never target-gated). **Exception,
matching the engine:** 4a(vi) forked-orphan-link findings are NOT target-gated —
`forkedOrphanTargets` deliberately reports orphans in untargeted roots
(`links.go:403`) and doctor must agree with the `StateUpgrade` those orphans force.
The stage-dir scan (4b) is target-independent: a staged copy for an untargeted
runtime belongs to a discovered skill and is NOT an orphan. Gating fixture: run
doctor with `SKILL_INSTALL_TARGETS=claude` and assert a dangling link under
`~/.cursor` **for a skill that ships a cursor overlay** produces no 4a finding while
the same link under `~/.claude` does — and that a leftover `~/.cursor` link for a
cursor-LESS skill still produces the 4a(vi) orphan finding despite the gating.

- **4a Target-link health**: fixtures for (i) dangling symlink at a target, (ii) live
  foreign symlink, (iii) stale staged copy behind our link, (iv) real dir at a target
  whose content differs from the repo (TargetStale), (v) **owned-legacy link** — a
  symlink pointing at the repo source or the legacy staged path (`ownedSources`
  membership, `links.go:389`) → its own finding class "legacy link; reinstall
  migrates it", NOT reported as foreign (the installer migrates these without force
  and a "foreign symlink" warning would be a false alarm on every pre-migration
  machine), (vi) **forked-orphan link** — the same `forkedOrphanTargets` source
  slice 2 reports as `orphan` → finding "orphan link for runtime without overlay"
  (keeps doctor consistent with `skills list` showing `upgrade` for the same skill).
  Findings must distinguish dangling from foreign-live (doctor does its own
  `Readlink`+`Stat`). Explicit non-findings: healthy installed, not-installed, and
  `TargetCopy` (real dir whose content matches — SkillState treats it as installed;
  doctor agrees).
  **Dedup rule**: a stale staged copy makes every root's link read `TargetStale`
  AND produces a 4b staged-drift finding for the same root cause. Doctor suppresses
  the per-target stale findings when the skill already has a staged-drift finding
  for the same staged copy, and the staged-drift detail names the affected targets —
  one finding per root cause, verified by a fixture asserting exactly one finding
  for a **third-party** skill staled across three roots (third-party skills share
  ONE staged copy across roots; a forked skill staled via a `shared/` edit has one
  staged copy per runtime and correctly yields one finding per staged copy).
- **4b Staged-copy drift & orphans**: (i) staged copy differing from repo source →
  `staged-drift` finding (reuse `pathsMatch`/`pathsMatchAssembled`), with a fixture
  covering a **forked team** (staged under `runtimes/<rt>/agent-teams/<teamDir>`,
  `RuntimeTeamStagedSource`, `stage.go:48`) as well as a portable skill; (ii)
  directory under the stage roots (`skills/`, `runtimes/<rt>/skills/`,
  `runtimes/<rt>/agent-teams/`, `agent-teams/`, `hooks/`) that is neither the
  `StagedSource` nor the legacy staged path
  (`stage.go:38`) of any discovered skill → orphan finding. This includes a staged
  copy under a runtime root whose skill no longer ships that overlay (e.g. a
  leftover `runtimes/cursor/skills/product-manager`): name-matching a discovered
  skill does not rescue a path that no current overlay stages — detail says "staged
  copy for a runtime this skill no longer targets; safe to remove". `backups/` is
  always ignored. A legacy-path staged copy of a discovered forked package is
  reported as staged-drift ("legacy staged copy; reinstall migrates it"), not
  orphan — this covers BOTH legacy forms: `skills/<name>` for forked portable
  skills (`LegacyStagedPath`, `stage.go:38`) and `agent-teams/<teamDir>` for
  forked teams (`legacyTeamOwnedSources`, `links.go:446`, treats these as
  installer-owned migrate-in-place sources). The team case is the state every
  pre-fork install is in since 6d13134/#74, so it gets its own fixture: a
  discovered forked team with a flat `agent-teams/<teamDir>` staged copy yields a
  staged-drift finding, never an orphan/"safe to remove".
- **4c Hook settings drift**: (i) settings file lacks the manifest command → finding
  with the expected command; (ii) file has a *similar but different* command → finding
  showing both strings; (iii) hook script symlink dangling or pointing outside the
  staged copy → finding. Reuses `hookScriptTargetState`/`hookSettingsEntryPresent`;
  nothing new exported — doctor lives in the same package.
- **4d Repo-side validation**: (i) duplicate names across kinds — one finding with
  empty `skill` (it belongs to no single skill); the detail names every colliding
  `kind/name`; (ii) `hooks/<dir>`
  missing `install.sh` or `hook.json` (Discover only warns and skips — doctor rescans
  `hooks/` directly); (iii) first-party skill missing `shared/` or missing
  `runtimes/claude/SKILL.md` or `runtimes/codex/SKILL.md`. **Cursor overlays are
  optional** (`isForkedSkill` requires claude+codex only; the repo lint *enforces*
  cursor-less packages, e.g. `product-manager` and the forked teams), so their
  absence is never a finding; a present
  cursor overlay missing `SKILL.md` is. Acceptance test: `Doctor` against the real
  repo checkout returns zero repo findings (guards against false positives on the
  actual tree).

### Slice 5 — Kong skeleton (`main.go` rewrite begins)

- **Failing test** (rewrite `main_test.go` incrementally; legacy `parseFlags` tests
  are deleted here and their still-relevant assertions ported into slices 5–7; the
  existing `TestCLIInterruptedTUIExitCode` (exit 130) is KEPT and must stay green):
  (i) `run([])` with non-TTY → new "Not a terminal" message, exit 1; (ii)
  `run(["--help"])` → exit 0, output contains the subcommand names present so far
  (substring asserts only — Kong help is never golden-tested), and the `runTUI` stub
  was NOT invoked (pins the Exit-sentinel design); (iii) `run(["bogus"])` → exit 2,
  stderr mentions `bogus`; (iv) `run(["--repo","/nonexistent"])` and a walk-up
  failure from a non-repo cwd → exit 2; (v) missing `HOME` → exit 2; (vi) `run([])`
  with TTY stub → `runTUI` invoked (default-command dispatch).
- **Red**: new tests fail against the legacy parser.
- **Green**: `go get github.com/alecthomas/kong@latest` (pinned tag). CLI struct with
  plain-string `Repo` + existing `resolveRepo` (keep our richer walk-up + error
  message, now exit 2 on failure) and `Tui` as `cmd:"" default:"1" hidden:""` —
  **subcommands are added to the Kong struct incrementally, each in its own slice
  (6–8), so no invocable stub command ever exists**; the `--help` substring test
  grows one name per slice. `run()` keeps its
  `(args, stdout, stderr, getenv, isTTY) int` signature; Kong parser per call with
  `kong.Writers` + panic-sentinel `kong.Exit` as specified in the contract; parse
  error → stderr + exit 2. Shared context (resolved repo, `skills.Config`) built once
  after parse, passed to the selected command's `Run`.
- **Refactor**: delete `parseFlags`, `usageText`; keep `resolveRepo`/`isSkillsRepo`.
- **Note**: from this slice until slice 9 the three `./install.sh`-driving shell
  suites are expected-red (see "Suite status between slices").

### Slice 6 — `skills list`

- **Failing test**: against a temp repo+HOME fixture (one installed, one
  not-installed, one hook), `run(["list"])` prints one line per skill
  `<name>  <kind>  <state>` (aligned, stable order = Discover order) and exits 0;
  `run(["list","--json"])` output unmarshals into the documented schema with the
  expected states; a runtime error (unreadable repo) exits 1.
- **Green**: `listCmd.Run` = `Discover` + `Report` per skill + text/JSON renderers.
- **Refactor**: extract the text table writer for reuse by doctor.

### Slice 7 — `skills install` / `skills uninstall`

- **Failing tests** (temp repo+HOME, table-driven):
  - `install <name>` installs exactly that skill (assert symlink on disk), prints the
    status line, exit 0.
  - `install` bare and `install --all <name>` → `Validate()` usage error, exit 2,
    nothing changed on disk. Same for `uninstall`.
  - `install --all` plans every skill from a pre-apply snapshot (mirrors today's
    `--all`), prints the status block, exit 0 when clean.
  - `install <name>` against a blocked target (real dir planted) → `! ... blocked`
    line, exit 1, dir untouched.
  - `install <name> --force` overwrites that planted real dir via the destroy-plan
    path (`^ upgraded` line), exit 0; `install --all --force` does it fleet-wide.
  - A force-path failure (fixture: read-only parent blocks the relink) → exit 1
    (via `ApplyResult.Err` / resulting blocked state).
  - `install nosuch` → stderr names it, exit 1, other requested names still applied.
    `install dup` with planted duplicate → ambiguity error listing `first/dup` and
    `hook/dup`, exit 1.
  - `uninstall <name>` removes only installer-owned links (plant a foreign symlink →
    survives), exit 0; a failing uninstall (read-only parent) → exit 1 despite the
    parity `- removed` line; `uninstall --all` mirrors today's `--none`.
  - `SKILL_INSTALL_TARGETS=claude` honored: cursor/agents roots untouched; hooks still
    processed (not target-gated). The skipped-team assertion needs a separate
    sub-case with `SKILL_INSTALL_TARGETS=agents` and a Claude-only (non-hybrid)
    team — under `targets=claude` nothing is skipped (`TeamManaged`,
    `targets.go:44`, is true whenever claude is targeted): skipped team reported
    as skipped, exit 0.
- **Green**: both commands share a helper: resolve names (or take the full list for
  `--all`), snapshot states, build `[]ApplyPlan`, call
  `ApplyAllResults(plans, force, w)`, derive exit code (any Blocked/Partial outcome,
  any `Err != nil`, or any resolve error → 1). `applyNoninteractive` and its bypass
  force loop are deleted; the old `main_test.go` assertions that covered them are
  ported to these tests (adjusted for the new status-line output of the force path).
- **Refactor**: none beyond the shared helper.

### Slice 8 — `skills doctor`

- **Failing test**: clean fixture → "no problems found", exit 0; fixture with a
  planted dangling link + stale staged copy + hook drift → one line per finding
  grouped by check, exit 1; `--json` unmarshals to the documented schema.
- **Green**: `doctorCmd.Run` = `Discover` + `Doctor` + renderers.

### Slice 9 — The breaking cut: binary rename, install.sh, shell tests, docs

Single commit inside the same PR so no consumer observes a half-migrated state.

- **Failing tests first** (shell): edit `scripts/test-install.sh` to (i) invoke
  `./install.sh install --all`, `./install.sh install --all --force`,
  `./install.sh uninstall --all` (updating expected output lines: the force case now
  prints plan-based `^`/`+` status lines instead of bare `+ <name>`). **Exit-code
  flip**: the blocked-targets case (today `--all` at `test-install.sh:47`) now exits
  **1** under the new contract — the script runs under `set -euo pipefail`, so the
  invocation must capture the status (`status=0; ./install.sh install --all || status=$?`)
  and assert `status -eq 1` before grepping the blocked line; the clean-install and
  force cases assert exit 0 the same way; (ii) expect the
  fake-go build command `build -o bin/skills .`; (iii) assert `bin/skills` exists;
  (iv) add a go.sum-staleness case: touch `go.sum`, rerun `install.sh`, assert a
  rebuild happens (fake-go invoked again); (v) smoke checks: `./install.sh list`
  exits 0 and mentions a known skill; `./install.sh doctor` exits 0 or 1 (never 2).
  Edit `test-hooks-install.sh` (×4 invocations) and `test-forked-skills-install.sh`
  (×1) the same way. Run → red against the old entry point.
- **Green**: `install.sh` — build `bin/skills`, extend the staleness `find` to
  include `go.sum`, exec `"$BIN" --repo "$REPO_DIR" "$@"`, update the header comment.
  All three shell suites green.
- **Docs** (all consumers, per the fixed decision): `AGENTS.md` Installation +
  Verification sections (subcommand surface, binary name, removed flags, exit codes,
  `--json`); **README.md** — the non-interactive flag docs (:109-111), the `--all`
  example (:106), and the `--force` mention in the TUI-apply description (:89) all
  get subcommand equivalents; `CLAUDE.md` is a symlink to `AGENTS.md` — edit
  `AGENTS.md` only. Grep sweep before committing:
  `rg -n -- 'skills-tui|--all|--none|--force' README.md AGENTS.md install.sh scripts/`
  (no `docs/` — the repo has none) and disposition every hit.
- **Verification**: run all five verification commands; manually run `./install.sh`
  (no args) in a real terminal once to confirm the TUI still launches.

## Risks & mitigations

- **Kong exit-flow**: Kong's help/version paths call the injected Exit and then
  return; a no-op Exit would fall through into default-command dispatch. Mitigated by
  the panic-sentinel design in the contract, pinned by slice-5 test (ii) asserting
  `--help` exits 0 without invoking `runTUI`.
- **Kong help brittleness**: substring asserts only; never golden-test Kong output.
- **`--force` semantics change** (today bare `--force` bypasses planning and
  reinstalls everything; new `install --force` is plan-based and only acts where the
  plan says so): behavior difference is intentional, documented in Non-goals, and
  every consumer script's expectations are rewritten and re-run in slice 9; old flags
  fail loudly with exit 2.
- **Exit-code coverage**: uninstall failures and force-path failures are only
  observable through `ApplyResult.Err` — added in slice 3 with dedicated tests, and
  exercised again at the CLI layer in slice 7.
- **Doctor false positives**: cursor-optional rule (slice 4d) plus the
  real-repo-checkout acceptance test; orphan detection excludes `backups/` and legacy
  staged paths of discovered skills; targets-gating rule stated explicitly with a
  fixture varying `SKILL_INSTALL_TARGETS`.
- **go.sum staleness gap in install.sh**: fixed and *tested* in slice 9 (iv).
- **Fixture cost in CLI tests**: reuse the package's existing temp-repo/temp-HOME
  helpers; lift them into a shared `testsupport_test.go` (or a tiny internal
  test-only package if `package main` tests need them) rather than duplicating.
- **Observation drift**: main moves fast in this repo (the draft plan went stale
  against two installer-touching merges during review); the slice-0 spot-check and
  the slice-9 grep sweep are the guards — treat cited line numbers as anchors.
- **govulncheck** now scans Kong: pin the latest tagged Kong release; if a
  vulnerability is flagged, stop and surface it rather than skipping the check.

## Stop conditions

- Any slice's red state doesn't match the prediction → stop and re-diagnose before
  implementing.
- TUI behavior regression (any existing `internal/tui` or `internal/skills` test
  breaks in a way that isn't an intentional seam change) → stop; the engine refactors
  (slices 2–3) must be behavior-preserving.
- Kong cannot cleanly express the specified dispatch (default TUI command +
  panic-sentinel exit) → stop and surface options (e.g. explicit pre-dispatch on
  `len(args)==0` before Kong parses) rather than contorting the CLI grammar.
- A real name collision exists in the repo today (slice 1 fixture is synthetic) →
  surface it; do not silently pick a kind.
- The slice-4d real-repo acceptance test finds a repo-level finding on a clean
  checkout → the check is wrong (false positive), not the repo; fix the check.

## Deliverable boundary

This plan produces no code. Implementation is a separate step (hand this plan to the
implementation workflow of choice, e.g. planned-implementation-agent or tdd).

title: Decide bd vs GitHub targets for issue-filing skills
description: Follow-up from the beads migration. The repo's issue tracker of record is now beads (prefix 'as'), but several portable skills file GitHub issues: write-a-prd (submits PRD as an issue), prd-to-issues, slice-issues. Decide per skill whether to add a bd mode, keep gh as the default (these are portable skills used across repos), or make the target configurable. Update skill docs and AGENTS.md conventions accordingly.

title: Live-smoke $feature-review subagent fan-out in Codex CLI
description:
Follow-up to #74. The codex overlay's parallel reviewer fan-out (`spawn_agent`/`wait`) is pinned by content tests but has not been exercised in a real Codex CLI session — prompt-driven spawning is model-mediated, so only a live run proves it triggers.

Steps:
1. Install via `./install.sh` so `~/.agents/skills/feature-review` points at the codex assembly.
2. In a repo with a small open PR, run `$feature-review <PR number>` in Codex CLI (current release; subagents are default-on).
3. Confirm: reviewers spawn in parallel (visible as subagent threads), each reads its checklist from the installed skill dir, no reviewer attempts writes or nested spawning, and the consolidated severity-tiered verdict report renders.
4. Also run once with a restricted focus (e.g. `$feature-review <PR> safety,quality`) and confirm only those reviewers spawn.
5. Force the fallback (e.g. decline the spawn or restrict approvals) and confirm the sequential inline path engages and says so in the report.

File fixes back against the overlay (`agent-teams/feature-review-team/runtimes/codex/SKILL.md`) if the fan-out doesn't trigger reliably — likely candidates are more explicit spawn wording or checklist-path handling.

title: Ship native Codex agent roles for feature-review reviewers
description:
Follow-up to #74. The feature-review codex overlay fans out reviewers via prompt-driven `spawn_agent` calls, carrying the read-only constraint and checklist path in each spawn prompt. Codex also supports registered agent roles — TOML files in `~/.codex/agents/` (or project `.codex/agents/`) with `name`, `description`, `developer_instructions`, and optional `model`, `model_reasoning_effort`, `sandbox_mode` (docs: https://developers.openai.com/codex/subagents).

Registered roles would upgrade the reviewers from prompt-carried constraints to enforced ones:

- `sandbox_mode = "read-only"` per reviewer instead of a prompt instruction.
- Pinned reviewer model (GPT-5.4 Mini is officially positioned for subagents) and `model_reasoning_effort`, mirroring the Claude agent frontmatter (`model: opus`, `effort: high`) the codex path currently loses.
- The shared checklist files are already shaped to become `developer_instructions` bodies.

Scope:
- Decide the source layout (e.g. `agent-teams/feature-review-team/runtimes/codex/codex-agents/*.toml`) and generation vs. hand-authoring from the shared checklists (watch for drift — a repo test should pin that each TOML tracks its checklist).
- New installer link family: stage and link the TOMLs into `~/.codex/agents/` (a root the installer does not currently manage; consider how it interacts with `SKILL_INSTALL_TARGETS`, uninstall, and the hooks precedent for `~/.codex` writes).
- Update the codex overlay to spawn by role name when the roles are installed, keeping the prompt-driven path as fallback for installs without them.
- Extend `scripts/test-forked-skills-install.sh` and AGENTS.md accordingly.

Deliberately out of scope for #74 because of the new installer surface.

title: Migrate go-review-team to the runtime-forked agent-team layout
description:
Follow-up to #74, which added runtime-forked agent-team support to the installer (`shared/` + `runtimes/{claude,codex}/`, per-runtime staged assemblies, claude agent-file links, legacy-link migration) and ported `feature-review-team` onto it.

`go-review-team` intentionally stayed the flat hybrid shape (root `SKILL.md` with `Platform — Claude Code` / `Platform — Codex` blocks plus root `agents/openai.yaml`). Migrate it onto the forked machinery so both teams share one shape:

- Move the four reviewer checklists (`structure-reviewer.md`, `error-reviewer.md`, `style-reviewer.md`, `security-reviewer.md`) to `shared/`, and `SKILL.md` + `review-lead.md` to `runtimes/claude/` (pure renames — Claude behavior unchanged).
- Write a `runtimes/codex/SKILL.md` modeled on feature-review's codex overlay: lead workflow with parallel read-only reviewer fan-out via the native subagent tools (`spawn_agent`/`wait`) built from the shared checklists, sequential inline fallback, no Platform blocks, no Claude-only tokens. Move `agents/openai.yaml` to `runtimes/codex/agents/openai.yaml` and consider `policy.allow_implicit_invocation: false`.
- Update repo tests: `TestRepoTeamsAreHybridGoReviewFlatFeatureReviewForked` currently pins go-review as flat/unforked and `TestGoReviewSkillDeclaresCodexWorkflow` asserts the Platform-block shape — both need replacing with forked-shape assertions (mirror the feature-review ones in `tools/skills-tui/internal/skills/repo_test.go`).
- The layout/install shell tests (`scripts/test-forked-skills-layout.sh`, `scripts/test-forked-skills-install.sh`) pick up forked teams automatically; add go-review-specific assertions where feature-review has them.
- Update AGENTS.md/README.md — after this lands, the flat-hybrid team shape has no remaining instances and its documentation can be simplified.

No installer engine changes should be needed; #74's machinery is team-generic.

title: Remove cursor overlays from runtime-forked skills; let Cursor consume the claude overlay
description:
## Background

Cursor (verified against 3.9.16 by reading the shipped `cursor-agent-exec` bundle) discovers skills from **all** of `~/.claude/skills`, `~/.codex/skills`, `~/.agents/skills`, and `~/.cursor/skills`, then deduplicates same-named skills first-wins in that scan order. With our default install targets (`agents,claude,cursor`), the `~/.claude/skills` copy always wins, so Cursor executes the **claude overlay** of every runtime-forked first-party skill. The cursor overlays we assemble and link into `~/.cursor/skills` are never loaded — they are dead weight. Details are in the "Cursor cross-root skill discovery" section of AGENTS.md.

## Decision

Remove the cursor overlays entirely and let Cursor load the claude overlay, which is what it already does:

- Delete `runtimes/cursor/` from all runtime-forked first-party skills.
- **Do not water down the claude overlays.** They remain Claude Code-native (Agent tool, `AskUserQuestion`, `$ARGUMENTS`, `disallowed-tools`, plan mode, etc.). Cursor is responsible for degrading gracefully on instructions it cannot follow.
- Forked skills stop installing anything into `~/.cursor/skills`; third-party (single-source) skills keep their existing three-root linking.

## Scope of work

- [ ] Delete `skills/*/runtimes/cursor/` for all 16 forked skills.
- [ ] `tools/skills-tui/`: stop assembling `~/.skill-symlinks/runtimes/cursor/skills/<name>` and stop linking `~/.cursor/skills/<name>` for forked skills (the `cursor` target should become a no-op for forked skills while still managing third-party skill links). See `internal/skills/links.go` (`SkillLinks`/`targetRuntime`) and discovery.
- [ ] Uninstall/cleanup: applying the installer should remove existing installer-owned `~/.cursor/skills/<name>` symlinks that point at staged cursor runtime copies of forked skills, and the now-orphaned staged copies under `~/.skill-symlinks/runtimes/cursor/`.
- [ ] Update tests: `scripts/test-forked-skills-layout.sh` (forked shape becomes `shared/` + `runtimes/{claude,codex}/`), `scripts/test-forked-skills-install.sh`, and the Go module tests (`scripts/test-skills-tui-go.sh`).
- [ ] Update AGENTS.md: Current Layout, staging table, "Cursor cross-root skill discovery" section, and the Conventions bullets that reference `runtimes/{claude,codex,cursor}/SKILL.md` and Cursor overlays.

## Non-goals

- No changes to claude or codex overlay content.
- No changes to third-party skill installation (they still link into `~/.cursor/skills`; Cursor dedups them against the `~/.claude/skills` copy harmlessly).
- No attempt to influence Cursor's precedence (e.g. via undocumented `metadata.surfaces` frontmatter).

title: skills-tui installer: build robustness (GOWORK, Go floor, offline vendoring) — PR #57 follow-ups
description:
## Summary

PR #57 ports the skills-tui installer to Go. During review, the Codex connector raised a cluster of **installer build-robustness** findings (offline builds, older Go toolchains, Go workspace mode). A vendoring-based fix set was implemented and then **deliberately reverted** by the maintainer (the three standalone bug fixes — `--repo` validation, cyclic-symlink comparison, FIFO rejection — were kept). These robustness findings remain open against the current tree at `52605d6`+.

This issue tracks them with an impact/severity evaluation so they can be triaged as deliberate decisions rather than auto-applied.

> Context: known-good implementations of all three exist in the reverted commits (`d5a69a2` vendoring, `8112786` Go-floor relax, `52e515e`/`1f52d1a` GOWORK guards) if any are re-adopted. `AGENTS.md` currently makes **no** offline/vendor/toolchain claims, so there is no documentation contradiction today.

## Findings & severity

| # | Finding | Severity | Trigger | Impact |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Missing `GOWORK=off` guard on Go build sites | **P2 (High)** for `install.sh`; P3 for test scripts | `GOWORK` env set, or checkout under a parent `go.work` that excludes `tools/skills-tui` | `go build` runs in workspace mode and fails before the installer/tests start |
| 2 | Module Go floor pinned to `go 1.25.0` | **P2 (Medium-High)** | Local Go toolchain < 1.25 | `GOTOOLCHAIN=local` refuses to build; default `GOTOOLCHAIN=auto` downloads a 1.25 toolchain (needs network) |
| 3 | External `x/term` dep is not vendored | **P2 (Medium)** — *needs a decision* | Fresh checkout with no module cache / no proxy | `./install.sh` must download `x/term` + `x/sys` before the TUI starts; fails offline/air-gapped |

### 1 — `GOWORK=off` guard (workspace-mode isolation)

The connector flagged two sites, but the class affects **all four** Go build invocations, none of which set `GOWORK=off`:

- `install.sh:18` — **user-facing install path** ([r3525468917](https://github.com/brian-bell/agent-skills/pull/57#discussion_r3525468917)) — this is the high-impact one: a user with a Go workspace configured cannot install skills at all.
- `scripts/test-install-skills.sh:125` ([r3525348307](https://github.com/brian-bell/agent-skills/pull/57#discussion_r3525348307)) — dev/CI only.
- `scripts/test-skills-tui-parity.sh:27` — dev/CI only (not yet flagged).
- `scripts/test-skills-tui-go.sh:14-16` (`go vet`/`build`/`test`) — dev/CI only (not yet flagged).

**Severity rationale:** `install.sh` is P2-High because it breaks the headline feature under a realistic, non-exotic condition (Go workspaces are common for multi-module developers), with a one-token fix. The test-script sites are P3 (no user impact). Reproduced previously: an unguarded `go vet` under a bogus `GOWORK` exits 1.

**Fix:** prefix each build with `GOWORK=off` (or `export GOWORK=off` once per script), mirroring the reverted `52e515e`/`1f52d1a`.

### 2 — Go version floor ([r3525468914](https://github.com/brian-bell/agent-skills/pull/57#discussion_r3525468914))

`go.mod` declares `go 1.25.0` (pulled in by `x/term v0.44.0`, which requires 1.25). Go 1.25 is recent, so machines on 1.24/older either refuse (`GOTOOLCHAIN=local`) or silently fetch a toolchain (`auto`), defeating a checkout-local install.

**Severity rationale:** P2 Medium-High — user-facing friction/failure for anyone not yet on Go 1.25, no workaround short of upgrading Go or going online.

**Fix:** lower the `go` directive (1.23 is a safe floor — the non-vendored source uses no >1.21 language features) and downgrade `x/term`→`v0.33.0` / `x/sys`→`v0.35.0` (both declare `go 1.23.0`), as in the reverted `8112786`.

### 3 — Vendoring for offline installs ([r3525468910](https://github.com/brian-bell/agent-skills/pull/57#discussion_r3525468910))

`install.sh` runs `go build` on first use, so a fresh checkout without module-cache/proxy access must download `x/term` + `x/sys` before any skill can be installed.

**Severity rationale:** P2 Medium — affects only offline/air-gapped/proxied environments; no doc currently promises offline. **This one was explicitly reverted by the maintainer**, so it is a conscious trade-off and should be a decision, not an automatic fix.

**Options:**
- **(a) Accept & document** network-on-fresh-checkout as an install requirement (smallest footprint; closes the gap by scoping the promise).
- **(b) Re-vendor** `x/term`/`x/sys` under `tools/skills-tui/vendor/` (restores offline; adds a vendored tree to the repo — the path that was reverted).
- **(c) Drop the dependency** — replace `x/term` usage (raw mode + size) with a small internal `termios`/`ioctl` shim, removing the external module entirely (no vendor tree, no download, no Go-floor pressure from `x/term`).

## Interaction

Findings 2 and 3 (plus the `GOWORK` guard) together decide whether "`./install.sh` builds from the checkout alone" holds. If option 3(c) is taken, it also resolves finding 2's `x/term`-driven Go floor. Worth deciding 3 first, since the choice shapes 2.

## Recommendation

- Do finding 1 (`GOWORK=off`) now — pure robustness, no trade-off, one-token fix across the 4 sites.
- Decide finding 3 (accept/vendor/drop-dep) — it gates finding 2.
- Apply finding 2's floor relax unless 3(c) removes `x/term` altogether.

_Source: unresolved review threads on PR #57 after the vendoring revert._

title: Restructure product-manager as an orchestrator-subagent skill (Claude + Codex overlays, cursor-less by design)
description:
## Goal

Restructure the `product-manager` skill into an **orchestrator–subagent** pattern: the main thread acts as the PM lead (owns user checkpoints, synthesis, and the final brief) and dispatches role subagents — a codebase surveyor, four dimension researchers, and a pre-delivery brief critic — whose prompts live once in `shared/roles/` and are consumed by the runtime overlays.

**Cursor policy (deliberate):** the skill ships **Claude and Codex overlays only**. Cursor discovers `~/.claude/skills` as a compat root and will consume the Claude overlay directly. We do not water down the Claude skill to accommodate Cursor — it stays fully Claude-native (`AskUserQuestion`, `Agent`, `SendMessage`, workflow mode), and Cursor deals with that as best it can. This makes the cursor overlay **optional repo-wide**, which requires installer changes (below).

## Background — verified platform capabilities (2026-07-08)

- **Codex** (developers.openai.com/codex): subagents are GA and default-on (`features.multi_agent = true`, ~v0.115, Mar 2026) — spawn / `wait_agent` / close, default limits 6 threads / depth 1, workers inherit sandbox and can use skills. Codex only fans out when the prompt/skill text explicitly says so. Web search default-on (cached). `request_user_input` is plan-mode-only, so checkpoints must be plain-chat questions. Skills are canonically discovered from `~/.agents/skills` (our existing install target).
- **Cursor** (cursor.com/docs): scans `~/.cursor/skills`, `~/.agents/skills`, **and** `~/.claude/skills` (+ legacy `~/.codex/skills`) for SKILL.md — this is what makes the cursor-less policy viable.
- The AGENTS.md delegation convention ("Codex may use subagents only when… a documented safe mechanism exists") is stale — the mechanism is now official and default-on. The convention gets rewritten as part of this change.

## Design

### Roles

| Role | Phase | Cardinality |
|---|---|---|
| Orchestrator (main thread) | all — scope, both checkpoints, gap analysis, ICE ranking, GTM, brief assembly | 1, never delegated |
| `codebase-surveyor` | 1 | 1 (2 for large repos: "architecture & maturity" / "distribution & CI signals") |
| `researcher` | 2 | 4 (competitors, trends, pain points, distribution) — parallel |
| `brief-critic` | 6 | 1, max two rounds |

Shared role contract (each file in `shared/roles/`): **Inputs** (fenced context block the orchestrator fills), **Conduct** (read-only, no further delegation, source-quality rules, domain briefs/rubric), **Output** (structured markdown, per-finding confidence, Sources; critic returns `blocker`/`fix`/`note` findings). Every role brief opens with "you start with no prior conversation context" — required by clean-context subagents and correct everywhere. All roles are leaf workers (Codex depth-1 forbids nesting anyway).

### Overlays

- **Claude** — keeps current mechanics: `Explore` survey, four named background researchers (`competitor-research`, `market-trends`, `pain-points`, `distribution-channels`) launched in one message, `SendMessage` deep-dive continuation, `AskUserQuestion` checkpoints, opt-in workflow mode. Prompts now built from `roles/*.md`; `runtimes/claude/research-agent.md` slims to a workflow-mode JSON-schema addendum (filename kept for the layout-test assertion). Phase 6 adds the brief-critic gate.
- **Codex** — real orchestrator: imperative spawn language ("spawn one agent per dimension, wait for all, consolidate" — Codex requires explicit instruction), 4-way parallel research within the 6-thread default, plain-chat checkpoints, cached-web-search staleness note, and an honest inline fallback (sequential role-passes, never described as delegation) for surfaces where spawning is unavailable or worker approvals would fail (`codex exec`).
- **Cursor** — none. Removed.

## Implementation checklist

### Phase A — orchestrator restructure (skill content)

- [ ] `shared/roles/researcher.md` — runtime-neutral move of the base prompt, four domain briefs with search queries, and markdown output format out of `runtimes/claude/research-agent.md`; token-clean (no `WebSearch`/`SendMessage`/etc.).
- [ ] `shared/roles/codebase-surveyor.md` — formalizes the Phase 1 survey (six identification bullets, focus split, read-only).
- [ ] `shared/roles/brief-critic.md` — QA rubric (traceability, genericity, ICE = Impact × Confidence × Ease arithmetic, effort grounding, ≤10 features, distribution-follows-persona), severity-tagged findings.
- [ ] `runtimes/claude/research-agent.md` → workflow-mode schema addendum only.
- [ ] Rewrite `runtimes/claude/SKILL.md` and `runtimes/codex/SKILL.md` per the design (add "Roles And Dispatch" section; phases dispatch shared roles; keep frontmatter, HARD-GATE, scope args, Common Mistakes, Red Flags).
- [ ] `scripts/test-forked-skills-layout.sh` (write first, TDD red): assert the three role files exist; scan `skills/product-manager/shared/` for Claude-only tokens; assert claude + codex overlays reference `roles/researcher.md`.

### Phase B — cursor-less forked skills (installer + policy)

- [ ] Delete `skills/product-manager/runtimes/cursor/`.
- [ ] `tools/skills-tui/internal/skills/discover.go`: `isForkedSkill` requires claude + codex overlays only; add `hasRuntimeOverlay` helper.
- [ ] `tools/skills-tui/internal/skills/links.go`: `SkillLinks` skips a targeted runtime root whose overlay is missing (factor the root table into a shared `portableRoots` var).
- [ ] `links.go`: prune **owned** orphaned runtime links on install and uninstall (`pruneOrphanedForkedLinks`) — otherwise a machine that installed before the overlay removal keeps `~/.cursor/skills/product-manager` pointing at a stale staged copy forever, and Cursor would load that instead of the Claude skill. Foreign symlinks/real paths untouched.
- [ ] Go tests (TDD red first): discovery marks a cursor-less skill Forked and rejects missing claude/codex; SkillLinks emits no cursor link; install prunes an owned orphan cursor link but leaves a foreign symlink; uninstall prunes the orphan too.
- [ ] `scripts/test-forked-skills-layout.sh`: require claude + codex overlays only; guard the codex/cursor token scan on directory existence; assert product-manager has **no** cursor overlay (cements the decision).
- [ ] `scripts/test-forked-skills-install.sh`: per skill, assert cursor staging + link only when `runtimes/cursor/SKILL.md` exists in the repo; otherwise assert no `~/.cursor/skills/<name>` link and no cursor staged dir; guard the codex/cursor staged-tree loops (Claude-only-file leak check, token scan, brief-template loop) the same way.

### Phase C — docs

- [ ] `AGENTS.md` (source of truth; `CLAUDE.md` is a symlink): layout section — cursor overlay is optional; when absent, no `~/.cursor` link is managed and Cursor consumes the Claude skill via its `~/.claude/skills` compat scan.
- [ ] `AGENTS.md` conventions — rewrite the delegation bullet: Codex native subagents (GA, default-on) may be directed by skill text with explicit spawn instructions, default limits respected, honest inline fallback, no false delegation claims; subagent prompts self-contained. Add the cursor policy: don't write watered-down cursor overlays; omit the overlay instead.
- [ ] `AGENTS.md` inventory — descriptor line for `product-manager`; installation table note for cursor-less forked skills.
- [ ] `README.md` — one-liner update.

### Verification

- [ ] `scripts/test-skills-tui-go.sh` (gofmt, vet, build, go test)
- [ ] `scripts/test-forked-skills-layout.sh`
- [ ] `scripts/test-forked-skills-install.sh`
- [ ] `scripts/test-install.sh`
- [ ] Temp-HOME `./install.sh --all`: claude/codex staged copies contain `roles/`; **no** `~/.cursor/skills/product-manager` link; pre-seeded owned cursor symlink gets pruned.
- [ ] Smoke: `/product-manager competitors` in Claude Code; `$product-manager competitors` in Codex CLI ≥ v0.115 (watch `/agent` fan-out); confirm Cursor picks up the skill from `~/.claude/skills`.

## Status

Partial implementation exists locally on worktree branch `worktree-product-manager-orchestrator` (uncommitted): Phase A is complete and green (layout/install/Go suites pass; temp-HOME install verified before the cursor removal); Phase B Go changes + tests are complete and green and the cursor overlay is deleted; Phase B shell-test updates and Phase C docs are outstanding.

## Risks / notes

- Relaxing the Codex delegation convention affects future overlays repo-wide — review that hunk deliberately.
- Cursor multi-root discovery has no documented dedup rule; a separate investigation task is tracking whether other first-party skills' triple-installed copies collide in Cursor. This issue's policy (no cursor overlay for product-manager) reduces the copies to two (claude via `~/.claude/skills`, codex via `~/.agents/skills`); Cursor's pick between those is deliberately left to Cursor.
- Parallel research multiplies tokens (~4-5×); the single-dimension scope argument remains the cheap path.

title: Remove the legacy installer (scripts/install-skills.sh) entirely
description:
## Why

With #63 / PR #67, every first-party skill is runtime-forked — and `scripts/install-skills.sh` is not fork-aware. Review of PR #67 reproduced the current behavior: the legacy installer discovers every `skills/*` directory as a first-party skill with no root-`SKILL.md` check, stages autobuild (and all 16 other forked skills) as broken `shared/ + runtimes/` copies that no runtime can load, symlinks them into `~/.agents/skills`, `~/.claude/skills`, and `~/.cursor/skills`, and exits 0 listing them as installed. Only third-party (root-`SKILL.md`) skills still install correctly through it.

The script is already deprecated (stderr warning, AGENTS.md says "retained only for old bash-path checks and does not support runtime-forked skills; use ./install.sh"). A deprecated entry point that silently produces broken installs for 17 of its ~25 targets is worse than no entry point. Remove it.

## Scope

- Delete `scripts/install-skills.sh`.
- Delete `scripts/test-install-skills.sh`, but **relocate the two tests in it that exercise the Go path**, not the legacy script:
  - `test_force_overwrites_existing_targets` (runs `./install.sh --force`)
  - `test_go_entrypoint_creates_missing_tui_bin_dir` (runs `./install.sh --none`)
  Move them into `scripts/test-skills-tui-go.sh` or a new `scripts/test-install.sh`.
- Update docs: drop `scripts/test-install-skills.sh` from the Verification lists in `AGENTS.md`/`README.md`; remove the "legacy `scripts/install-skills.sh` is retained…" sentences from AGENTS.md (Installation and Conventions sections).
- `scripts/skills-tui.sh` stays: it is documented as the behavioral spec/reference for the Go port and is exercised by `scripts/test-skills-tui.sh`. Only its consumer `install-skills.sh` goes away. (If the spec role is also considered obsolete, that's a separate decision.)
- Grep for remaining references (`rg install-skills`) after removal.

## Acceptance

- `scripts/install-skills.sh` and its test are gone; no repo references remain.
- The two Go-path tests still run in some suite and pass.
- `AGENTS.md`/`README.md` verification and conventions sections updated.
